items.title: Senior firefighter claims boss labelled her 'an overly emotional woman'

children.content: A former senior Northern Territory firefighter has claimed the service's deputy chief labelled her "an overly emotional woman" in a complaint to the territory's Anti-Discrimination Commission.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue uniform smiles for the camera.

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction

children.content: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

image.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

items.title: NT outlaw bikie gang president sentenced to 10 years in prison

children.content: Secretly taped phone calls have captured the former president of the Mongols bikie gang's Northern Territory chapter discussing interstate drug deals while helping supply methamphetamine worth almost $800,000 to customers across the Top End.

gallery.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer holds up a black vest reading Mongols MC Australia.

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

image.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

items.title: One CDU employee has been back paid $242k amid $4m stolen wages bill

children.content: Charles Darwin University (CDU) has been directed to back pay more than $4 million in wages after it was found to have underpaid more than 800 professional and academic staff members between 2016 and 2022.

image.altText: CDU sign

items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

gallery.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'
A white tunnel medical machine, white walls inside with blue sheets and blue art pieces on the wall.
A white man in black t-shirt inside a large white tunnel scanning machine, with a nurse in red shirt and black vest standing.

thumbnail.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'
A white tunnel medical machine, white walls inside with blue sheets and blue art pieces on the wall.
A white man in black t-shirt inside a large white tunnel scanning machine, with a nurse in red shirt and black vest standing.

items.title: Second Mongols bikie to escape custody in the NT caught in eight minutes

children.content: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

image.altText: The side door of a NT Police car.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

articles.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party

children.content: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

image.altText: Two women taking on radio into microphones.

articles.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

children.content: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

image.altText: Mike Burgess, Head of ASIO, generic

articles.title: 'ISIS bride' barred from Australia issued permit to return

children.content: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

children.content: The government's signature budget bill, which includes changes to capital gains tax, negative gearing and a Working Australian Tax Offset (WATO), has passed federal parliament. Follow live.

items.title: Labor's CGT, negative gearing budget bill passes federal parliament

updates.title: Labor's signature budget measures pass federal parliament
Treasurer confirms changes to tax treatment of jointly owned assets in future legislation
Senate passes the government's signature budget bill

items.title: Big battery boom means fewer transmission lines will be needed

children.content: Home batteries hit 600,000 as households play big role in energy transition, but building transmission projects remains "critical", according to the latest AEMO energy plan.

image.altText: Michael McGarvie carbon-gel battery system at his home in Eaglemont, on October 20, 2015.

items.title: 'Some cracks' appearing in jobs market, even as unemployment rate dips

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate slipped to 4.4 per cent in May, down from 4.5 per cent in April, in seasonally adjusted terms.

image.altText: Boxing Day sales Pitt Street mall Sydney

items.title: Sea bird sick with H5 avian flu landed by bodyboarders, too weak to fly to shore

children.content: A surfer who spotted the first bird in South Australia to test positive for H5N1 bird flu says the giant petrel looked fatigued and distressed before it was retrieved by wildlife rescuers.

image.altText: A man with white hair and a black hoodie smiles at the camera

items.title: Conspiracy theorist podcaster jailed for stabbing girlfriend to death with dagger

children.content: Tobias Nuttall is sentenced to at least 18 years behind bars for murdering his girlfriend with a 25-centimetre dagger in a "vicious" attack in her suburban Perth home.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man with long brown hair and a moustache, talking while appearing on a video call for a podcast.

items.title: Laptop at the pub or office watch party: How Australian workers plan to watch the Socceroos

children.content: The countdown is on for Australia's World Cup match against Paraguay, and some football fans are preparing to happily put work on the backburner.

articles.title: The calls for Australia to play its best attacking weapons are loud

children.content: Tony Popovic dances to his own selection tune, but that doesn't mean there aren't loud calls for him to select Australia's most attacking weapons against Paraguay.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

items.title: ABC fishing star rescued from rough seas after boat capsizes

children.content: The voice of John "Stinker" Clarke is well known to those who love to wet a line, but there was no-one around to hear a cry for help when his boat capsized north of Newcastle.

items.title: Ghost nets transformed into art to highlight pollution's 'devastating' impact

children.content: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

items.title: Tribute to 'respected' WA pilot after fatal flight in plane he built himself

children.content: A friend of 76-year-old pilot Malcolm Vivian remembers him as a "beautiful man" and "special aircraft builder" after his death in a light plane crash near Northam, and says he does not think pilot error was to blame.

items.title: Taking the plunge in chilly winter water to tackle loneliness

children.content: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

image.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

items.title: Australians' tricks for dealing with heatwaves that Europeans yet to learn

children.content: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

items.title: Cairns mayor admits misconduct for sharing confidential information

children.content: Amy Eden says she accepts a tribunal's findings of misconduct against her for twice leaking confidential information to a journalist. But she has defended her decision to shut down councillors' questions about her admission in the council chamber.

items.title: Brisbane teen acquitted of planning terror attack targeting Peter Dutton

items.title: Can vaccines stop deadly bird flu from spreading?

children.content: With bird flu arriving on our shores, governments, wildlife groups and agricultural organisations are springing into action. But can vaccines limit the economic and environmental damage of the deadly influenza?

items.title: Olympian arrested at reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says

children.content: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

image.altText: People gather near the reflecting pool, including a person waving a large American flag.
